narrative.headline: What 2018 Hyundai Santa Fe owners complain about most
narrative.summary: Owners filed 108 complaints with NHTSA for the 2018 Hyundai Santa Fe. The engine draws by far the most reports, making up nearly half of all complaints, followed by the brakes. A handful of reports mention a fire or a crash, so look at the details before you buy.

issues.sample: DRIVING @60 W/CRUSE ON GOING DOWN A HILL ENGINE RPM'S SUDDENLY JUMP FROM 2000 TO ANYWHERE FROM 3000 TO 5000 RPM'S AND THE ONLY WAY TO STOP ENGINE RACING IS TO TAP THE BRAKES TO DISENGAGE CRUSE. ALSO VEHICLE WILL NOT SPEED UP WHEN TURNING A CORNER,LIKE THE TRANSMISSION DOESN'T DOW
issues.title: Engine
issues.blurb: The engine is the top complaint by a wide margin: 53 reports, about half of all complaints (49%). Three of those reports involved a fire.

issues.title: Service Brakes
issues.blurb: Brakes are the second most reported area, with 19 reports (18%). These include 2 crashes and 2 injuries.

issues.title: Hydraulic
issues.blurb: The hydraulic system accounts for 12 reports (11%). No crashes, fires, or injuries were reported in this group.

issues.title: Power Train
issues.blurb: The power train shows up in 10 reports (9%), including 1 crash and 1 injury.

issues.title: Electrical System
issues.blurb: The electrical system rounds out the list with 9 reports (8%), 2 of which involved a fire.
